one_line_summary: PRED-105 silent-ignore advanced to 12 consecutive polls (~179.1h / 7.46d past close); the 4th consecutive ECM Daily Review auto-regen FIRED (06:46:57Z, 8ee53c3) and HELD at registry_ratio_pct=94.5 / confirmed=69 / prospective=10 / refined=4. The pipeline-canonical silent-ignore is now a stable 4-day streak across daily_review regens, not in-flight buffering. The pre-locked FAIL-009 stat-refresh rubric the prior analyst designed has fired; the prior 06:04Z stat-refresh proposal was deferred by the decider per option (b), and the next material event (4th-regen verdict) has now resolved.

poll_data.daily_review_auto_regen_today: FIRED 2026-04-25T06:46:57Z (8ee53c3); HELD at confirmed=69 / prospective=10 / refined=4 / registry_ratio_pct=94.5 — no PRED-105 absorption into refined
poll_data.threshold_state: 7-day manual-commit drought SUSTAINED past threshold; 8.0d crossing imminent at next poll

kernel_of_truth: A 4-consecutive auto-regen holding at refined=4 is exactly what the registry would do anyway if PRED-105 simply has not been adjudicated yet — i.e. the pipeline only updates the refined bucket when monitor.py / build_daily_review.py is given a positive signal of failure, and absent such a signal the count reflects "unadjudicated" rather than "silently ignored." The author may be on a break, may be running a repro experiment, may be drafting a writeup he has not yet pushed.
why_kernel_does_not_save_it: A 7.46-day-past-close prediction with no published outcome AND no public "still investigating" / "data delayed" note from the author IS silent-ignore by definition; the difference between "not yet adjudicated" and "quietly dropped" is exactly what observers cannot distinguish without an explicit author statement, and the absence of such a statement across 12 polls is itself the artifact our review documents. Moreover, the auto-regen would re-emit a refined counter incremented to 5 if monitor.py had any heuristic for detecting expired prospective windows; that heuristic does not exist, which is the structural finding. The pipeline-canonical silent-ignore is now a stable 4-day streak across regen ticks — it is no longer in-flight buffering of an in-progress adjudication, it is the steady-state behaviour of the autonomous pipeline.

Patches are tightly scoped substring-replacements; no prose restructure.

ISS-1423 was closed 2026-04-25T11:27:03Z by decider-self-apply. The underlying pattern continued past close, but reopening would oscillate the issue tracker. The new patch can ride under a fresh content-update issue or be applied directly by the decider.

FAIL-009 is therefore still carrying yesterday's numbers; the current poll provides the material-event resolution that justifies the deferred refresh now.

Per user preference (consider opposing arguments): this analysis foregrounds the on-break / awaiting-adjudication counterargument as the kernel of truth and rebuts it via the build_daily_review.py canonicalisation evidence. The 4th-regen-holds-at-4 outcome is the high-information signal that distinguishes "unadjudicated buffer" from "steady-state pipeline behaviour".

No newsworthy flag — the 4th-consecutive-regen and 7.96d-drought milestones are still jargon for non-specialists. Newsworthy escalation would require either (a) drought-break with no PRED-105 mention, (b) an explicit goalpost-move, or (c) refined=5 with PRED-105 absorption.
